message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_cummax / tensor_cummin autograd via argmax-routing backward [bd-wkkw]

tensor_cummax (lib.rs:10992) and tensor_cummin (lib.rs:11020) used
to extract values, compute cum_vals + cum_idx in plain f64, and
rebuild both as requires_grad=false leaves — silently severing
autograd through the values branch. PyTorch's torch.cummax /
torch.cummin are differentiable via argmax-routing.

Wrap the values branch in tensor_apply_function with the analytical
backward:
   grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]

cum_idx is computed in the forward (using a non-grad scan over
input values) and captured via closure so the backward uses the
exact same routing the forward used. The indices branch remains
a non-grad leaf (integer-valued, correctly non-differentiable).

Adds two regression tests:
  - cummax_propagates_gradient_via_argmax_routing
    x = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5] → cum_idx = [0, 0, 2, 2, 4]
    Under sum loss, grad = [2, 0, 2, 0, 1] (each grad_x[k] is the
    count of i values that route to k).
  - cummin_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_routing
    Mirror for cummin: x = [5, 3, 4, 1, 2] → grad = [1, 2, 0, 2, 0].

Closes frankentorch-wkkw. ft-api lib tests: 1161 passing (+2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -181,6 +181,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-w6l2","title":"tensor_block_diag is severed-autograd: linear backward [child of d180/cross/i4rd cluster]","description":"tensor_block_diag at lib.rs:3852 places each input tensor as a block on the diagonal of a larger zero-initialized matrix. PyTorch's torch.block_diag is differentiable. Forward is purely linear in each input — output[row, col] = block_k[r, c] when (row, col) is in block k's region, 0 otherwise.

Backward extracts each block from the corresponding region of grad_output:
   grad_block_k[r, c] = grad_output[row_offset_k + r, col_offset_k + c]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> backward that splits grad_output into per-input blocks. Shapes need to be reconstructed during backward; closure can capture (block_rows, block_cols, total_rows, total_cols) tuples.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_block_diag in tensor_apply_function with per-block extraction backward. Pre-computed (rows, cols, row_offset, col_offset, original_shape) for each input is captured by both the forward and backward closures so the same coordinate-mapping is used in both directions. Backward returns a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input, each containing the corresponding sub-region of grad_output. Added block_diag_propagates_gradient_through_each_block regression test (two 2x2 blocks A=[[1,2],[3,4]], B=[[5,6],[7,8]] in a 4x4 output, weighted-sum loss with weights 0..15 → grad_a = [0,1,4,5], grad_b = [10,11,14,15]). ft-api lib tests: 1146.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:20:38.093753739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:23:07.389119917Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:23:07.386727212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wd1","title":"Add functional batch norm API wrappers in ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:12:18.738793479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:18:33.991685617Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:18:33.991395117Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wfyq","title":"sign returns -1.0 for -0.0, diverges from torch.sign","description":"sign_scalar and sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 use Rust's f64::signum which returns -1.0 for -0.0 and 1.0 for 0.0 (sign-bit semantics). torch.sign returns 0.0 for both signed zeros. Existing tests at crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:9188-9206 document the Rust behavior as expected. Fix: replace .signum() with explicit branches that map both signed zeros to 0.0, propagate NaN, and return ±1.0 only for non-zero finite values. Update tests to assert torch parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T21:23:16.000385332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.951035096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.949171877Z","close_reason":"sign now matches torch.sign: both signed zeros map to +0.0, NaN propagates as NaN, and ±non-zero return ±1.0. F64 (sign_scalar/sign_tensor_contiguous_f64) and F32 (sign_tensor_contiguous_f32) all routed through new torch_sign_{f32,f64} helpers replacing Rust's IEEE-754-sign-bit signum. 4 new edge-case tests in ft-kernel-cpu + 2 stale ft-autograd tests fixed; ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-wkkw","title":"tensor_cummax / tensor_cummin sever autograd; argmax-routing backward needed","description":"tensor_cummax (lib.rs:10992) and tensor_cummin (lib.rs:11020) compute cumulative max/min along the flattened tensor and rebuild requires_grad=false leaves. PyTorch's torch.cummax / torch.cummin are differentiable.

Gradient flows back via argmax-routing: cum_max[i] takes its value from x[argmax_{j <= i} x[j]] = cum_idx[i]. So backward:
   grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]

The cum_idx tensor is already computed in the forward; saving it for backward avoids any extra work. Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward. Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cummax and tensor_cummin's values branch in tensor_apply_function with argmax-routing backward: grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]. The cum_idx is computed in the forward (using a non-grad scan) and captured by the closure so the backward uses the same routing the forward used. The indices branch remains a non-grad leaf (correct — indices are integer-valued).

Added cummax_propagates_gradient_via_argmax_routing and cummin_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_routing regression tests asserting analytical gradients on representative input ([3,1,4,1,5] and [5,3,4,1,2]). ft-api lib tests: 1161.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:52:13.928139046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:54:33.233711053Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:54:33.231366753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wq4","title":"Implement tensor_block_diag operation","description":"Add block_diag across the stack. Creates block diagonal matrix from provided tensors. PyTorch signature: torch.block_diag(*tensors). Used in structured ops and GNNs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:19.893738981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302493878Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302253813Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_block_diag with 1-D support, 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wze","title":"Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-xsp.

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

Artifacts:
- artifacts/beads_recovery/FRANKENTORCH_XSP_IMPORT_PATH_REPORT_20260407.md
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_probe_20260407T025948Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_import_focus_20260407T030054Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_repeat_import_20260407T030112Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_sequence_20260407T030136Z/results.json

Next work:
1. Escalate the import-path / blocked-cache page leak upstream in beads_rust or frankensqlite.
2. Preserve a local recovery playbook that warns agents to avoid auto-import on freshly vacuumed recovery DBs until the upstream fix lands.
3. Re-validate the workaround after the upstream fix by repeating the xsp probe matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.139752494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.555177457Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.554917796Z","close_reason":"Filed upstream escalation as beads_rust#224, expanded the archived import-path report with a concrete local recovery playbook, and added a temporary AGENTS.md warning to keep vacuumed recovery DB sessions on --no-auto-import until the fix lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
